title: Structured-Light 3D Imaging (projector-camera triangulation)
source_data_quality_note: Source MD is widefield-fluorescence template (fan-out artifact). Physics below is grounded in canonical structured-light literature (Salvi, Scharstein-Szeliski 2003; phase-shift + gray-code encoding).

E.description: A projector casts a sequence of known patterns onto the scene; a calibrated camera captures K images of the deformed patterns. For each pixel, decoding the pattern ID (Gray-code + phase-shift) assigns a projector column index; triangulating camera ray and projector plane via the calibrated epipolar geometry yields depth.
E.forward_model: I_k(u,v) = rho(u,v) * P_k(proj_col(u,v)) * max(0, n . L_proj) + I_ambient + n_k(u,v)
E.world_state_x: Depth map d(u,v) in R^{H x W} or full 3D mesh
E.observation_y: K pattern images y_k(u,v) in R^{H x W x K} (K = 8-32 for Gray-code + N-step phase-shift)
physical_parameters_theta: Projector resolution W_p x H_p, pixel pitch, focal length f_p
Projector-camera baseline B, extrinsic R|t, stereo rectification
Pattern set (Gray-code, N-step phase shift, random speckle, Hamiltonian)
Pattern wavelength lambda and ambient rejection

W.regime: Well-posed when K projector-pattern bits exceed log2(W_p) + phase-unwrap margin and surface is Lambertian. Degrades on specular, translucent, dark, and high-frequency surfaces (e.g. hair), in strong ambient light, and at occlusions (camera sees but projector cannot).

C.solver_class: Gray-code binary decode + phase-shift sub-pixel, phase-unwrap (TPU), robust decode (graph-cut), learned (SL-Net, DeepSL)
C.error_bound: sigma_d = (d^2 / (f * B)) * sigma_disparity; sigma_disparity ~ 1/(N_phase * sqrt(SNR)); ambient light reduces contrast quadratically
C.complexity: O(K * H * W) for decode; O(H * W) for triangulation
